output:
{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "SessionStart", "additionalContext": "## Rule: aem-local-instance.mdc # AEM local instance configuration When connecting to a local AEM instance (for build, deploy, toggle management, testing, or version detection), follow the **detect-aem-version** skill for discovery, version detection, and instance selection. Do not assume port 4502. Never use the first responding port without version detection. All details (port scan, run modes, product info, instance matching) are in the skill. When `AEM_HOST` is set in the environment, use it as the AEM hostname instead of scanning localhost. This is set automatically when running inside Docker with an AEM sidecar (`docker compose --profile aem`). --- ## Rule: github-api-formatting.mdc # GitHub API multi-line strings When calling any GitHub MCP tool that accepts multi-line text (PR body, issue body, comments), use **actual newlines** in the parameter value. Never use literal `\n` escape sequences — they render as visible `\n` on GitHub instead of line breaks. ``` BAD — "## Summary\n\nFixed the bug.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] tested" GOOD — "## Summary Fixed the bug. ## Test plan - [x] tested" ``` This applies to all GitHub tools: `create_pull_request`, `create_issue`, `add_issue_comment`, `update_issue`, etc. --- ## Rule: scope-discovery.mdc # Scope discovery — GitHub and Git.corp fallback **If the affected JCR path or file cannot be found locally**, do NOT ask the user. Search both sources, verify the candidate is active (not archived), and report the active repo URL. ## Search procedure (single source of truth) 1. **Discover MCP servers** from the project's `mcps/` folder. Identify: - **GitHub (GHEC):** server identifier containing `github` or `gh` (e.g. `user-github.com`) - **Git.corp (Adobe internal):** server identifier containing `git.corp` or `corp` (e.g. `user-git.corp.adobe.com`) 2. **Call `search_code`** on **both** sources (not fallback — parallel). Do not stop at the first hit: - **GitHub:** query `path:{PATH_SEGMENT} org:adobe` (e.g. `path:libs/mcm org:adobe`) - **Git.corp:** query `path:{PATH_SEGMENT}` (e.g. `path:libs/mcm`). AEM/Granite/MCM code was historically on Git.corp and is actively being migrated to GHEC. 3. **Verify archive status for each candidate** before selecting one. For every matching repo, call the source's repo-info tool (`search_repositories` with `org:X name:Y`, or equivalent) and read the `archived` field. - **Active repo found:** prefer it. Report `found on {source}: {URL}`. - **All candidates archived** on one source but the other source has an active match → select the active one. Report `found on {source}: {URL} (upstream on {other source} is archived)`. - **Only archived candidates exist anywhere:** do NOT silently accept them. An archived repo with a recent fix history almost always has a migrated successor. Search the **other** source for the successor using: - the same path segment, plus - the last-known org/repo name as a keyword (e.g. `ui-wcm-admin`), plus - recent PRs on the archived repo that reference the migrated location (check the README, pinned issues, or the last few commit messages for "moved to" / "migrated to" notes). 4. **If no active repo can be found anywhere**, report the archived URL **along with** `archived: true` and stop — do NOT fabricate a migrated URL and do NOT treat the archived repo as the fix target. The caller is responsible for surfacing this as a human-resolution item (see `skills/commit-and-pr/SKILL.md` — the archived-upstream guard). --- ## Rule: toolkit-metadata.mdc # Toolkit metadata When executing a skill, agent, rule, or command that has a `metadata.toolkit` field in its YAML frontmatter, follow these rules. ## 1. Agent window display At the start of each artifact activation, display to the user: ``` v — []() ``` Example: `commit-and-pr v2.1.0 — [cursor-toolkit](https://github.com/Adobe-AEM-Sites/aem-cursor-toolkit)` When a skill delegates to another skill, display the delegation: ``` -> v — []() ``` Example: `-> commit-and-pr v2.1.0 — [cursor-toolkit](https://github.com/Adobe-AEM-Sites/aem-cursor-toolkit)` ## 2. Commit trailers When creating git commits, append a trailer with the primary skill that performed the work: ``` Skill-Version: / v ``` Example: `Skill-Version: cursor-toolkit/fix-accessibility v1.0.0` ## 3. PR footers When creating pull request descriptions, append a footer listing all artifacts that were activated during the workflow, grouped by toolkit: **Single toolkit:** ``` Generated by [](): v, v ``` Example: `Generated by [cursor-toolkit](https://github.com/Adobe-AEM-Sites/aem-cursor-toolkit): fix-accessibility v1.0.0, commit-and-pr v2.1.0` **Multiple toolkits:** ``` Generated by [](): | [](): ``` ## Reading metadata Read all values from the active artifact's frontmatter: - `metadata.toolkit` — the toolkit name (namespace) - `metadata.repository` — the repository URL - `metadata.version` — the artifact's own version Use them as-is — do not hardcode or guess any values. Each artifact has its own independent version. If the active artifact does **not** have `metadata.toolkit`, do nothing — this rule only applies to artifacts that declare toolkit metadata. --- ## Rule: validation-after-fixes.mdc # Validation after fixes When you have applied code fixes (e.g. from a11y, security fixes, or similar): 1. **Run the repo's local build/lint** (e.g. `npm run prepack`, `npm run build`, `npm run lint`). Fix any errors before proceeding. 2. **Then** run any domain-specific validation (e.g. a11y checks, tests). Do not open a PR until both pass. If the repo uses a single script that runs build and lint (e.g. `prepack` in commons-style packages), run that and fix all failures. --- ## Rule: worktree-isolation.mdc # Worktree isolation A plain `git checkout ` mutates the **shared** working tree. If the user (or another agent) is working in the same repo in parallel, that checkout — and any later commit — collides with their state: branch switches yank files out from under each other, uncommitted edits get stashed or lost, and `git commit` fails with `cannot lock ref 'HEAD'`. When a workflow needs a branch other than the one currently checked out, **work in a dedicated worktree**, not the main checkout. ## When this applies - Skills that check out an existing branch to apply changes (e.g. `implement-review-findings` Step 4 checking out a PR's head branch). - Orchestrators and finalizers that create or switch branches (`commit-and-pr`, `resolve-jira-ticket`, `xss-fix-orchestrator`). - Any task the user is likely to run **in parallel** with other work in the same repo (multiple PR reviews, batch fixes). ## How to isolate Prefer the harness primitive when spawning a subagent: - `Agent` tool with `isolation: "worktree"` — the agent gets its own worktree, auto-cleaned if unchanged. Otherwise create one explicitly and operate inside it: ```bash wt="$(mktemp -d)/wt" git worktree add "$wt" # existing branch # or: git worktree add -b "$wt" # ... edit, stage, commit, push from inside "$wt" ... git worktree remove "$wt" ``` Stashes are repo-global, so a `git stash pop` works the same inside a worktree — useful when restoring parked edits. ## Do not - Do **not** `git checkout` a different branch in the main working tree when the user may be mid-task there. Detect the situation (`git branch --show-current` differs from the target, working tree dirty) and use a worktree instead. - Do **not** assume a failed commit (`cannot lock ref 'HEAD'`) means lost work — check `git stash list` and `git reflog` first; the edits are almost always recoverable. --- ## Rule: xss-prevention.mdc # XSS Prevention in AEM ## Critical rules 1. **AEMCS fixes (main/master) MUST be behind a Granite feature toggle** (`CT_SITES-XXXXX`). On-prem 6.5 / 6.5 LTS backports (release/650, release/660) do NOT use toggles — apply the encoding unconditionally. 2. **Fix in the specific affected file**, NOT in shared utilities 3. **Encode right before DOM insertion** — at the output point (constructor, onRender, initComponent, template) 4. **Fall back to the original value** if the toggle is disabled or the XSS API is unavailable (AEMCS only) ## Server-side (JSP) Always include `/libs/foundation/global.jsp` to get the `xssAPI` object. | Output context | Method | Example | |---------------|--------|---------| | HTML text content | `xssAPI.encodeForHTML(val)` | `<%= xssAPI.encodeForHTML(title) %>` | | HTML attribute | `xssAPI.encodeForHTMLAttr(val)` | `title="<%= xssAPI.encodeForHTMLAttr(title) %>"` | | URL / href | `xssAPI.getValidHref(url)` | `href="<%= xssAPI.getValidHref(link) %>"` | | JavaScript string | `xssAPI.encodeForJSString(val)` | `var x = '<%= xssAPI.encodeForJSString(val) %>';` | Never output raw `properties.get(...)` or request parameters without encoding. ```jsp <%@include file="/libs/foundation/global.jsp" %>

<%= xssAPI.encodeForHTML(title) %>

<%= xssAPI.encodeForHTML(title) %> ``` ## Client-side (JavaScript) **`Granite.Sling.XSS` is NOT available in Classic UI.** Classic UI uses `cq.widgets` clientlibs, not Granite clientlibs. In Touch UI / Granite UI pages, `Granite.Sling.XSS` IS available. ### Encoding APIs available in AEM | Context | API | Available in | |---------|-----|-------------| | String config → DOM (name, id, label) | `CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(val)` | Classic UI widget pages | | HTML content (rich markup) | `Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS.sanitizeHtml(val)` | Classic UI and Granite UI pages | | URL / iframe src | `sanitizeURL()` helper using `new URL(path, document.baseURI)` | Any JS context | | Single-quoted HTML attribute | `value.replace(/'/g, ''')` | Any JS context | | JSON response parsing (replace eval) | `JSON.parse(json)` or `CQ.Ext.decode(json)` | Any JS context | ### Feature toggle and availability guard boilerplate (AEMCS only) Feature toggles are required **only for AEMCS** (main/master branch). For 6.5 / 6.5 LTS backports, skip the toggle and apply the encoding unconditionally. ```js // AEMCS — wrap in toggle guard if (Granite && Granite.Toggles && Granite.Toggles.isEnabled("CT_SITES-XXXXX")) { // XSS fix here — replace XXXXX with the actual Jira ticket key } // 6.5 / 6.5 LTS — no toggle, apply directly cfg.name = CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(cfg.name); ``` Standard availability checks (AEMCS): ```js var toggleEnabled = Granite && Granite.Toggles && Granite.Toggles.isEnabled("CT_SITES-XXXXX"); var xssAvailable = CQ && CQ.shared && CQ.shared.XSS; var sanitizeHtmlAvailable = Granite && Granite.UI && Granite.UI.Foundation && Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS; if (toggleEnabled && xssAvailable) { cfg.name = CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(cfg.name); } ``` ## Fix patterns (from commit history) The patterns below show the AEMCS form (with toggle guard). For 6.5 / 6.5 LTS backports, remove the toggle check and apply the encoding unconditionally (keep the API availability check). ### Pattern 1: Sanitize config properties with CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue() For string config properties that get inserted into DOM (name, id, inputType, hrefTarget, prefix/suffix labels): ```js // AEMCS (with toggle) if (toggleEnabled && xssAvailable) { cfg.name = CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(cfg.name); } // 6.5 / 6.5 LTS (no toggle) if (xssAvailable) { cfg.name = CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(cfg.name); } ``` ### Pattern 2: Sanitize HTML content with Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS.sanitizeHtml() For properties that contain HTML markup (e.g. `this.html`, rich text fields): ```js // AEMCS (with toggle) if (toggleEnabled && sanitizeHtmlAvailable) { this.html = Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS.sanitizeHtml(this.html); } // 6.5 / 6.5 LTS (no toggle) if (sanitizeHtmlAvailable) { this.html = Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS.sanitizeHtml(this.html); } ``` ### Pattern 3: Sanitize URLs with a sanitizeURL helper For URLs loaded into iframes or used as src/href: ```js sanitizeURL: function(path) { try { var url = new URL(path, document.baseURI); if (url.origin !== "null") { return url.pathname + url.search; } else { return ""; } } catch(error) { return ""; } } ``` ### Pattern 4: Encode single quotes for HTML attribute context When values are interpolated into single-quoted HTML attributes (e.g. `src='...'`): ```js var encodeForHTMLAttr = function(value) { if (typeof value !== 'string') return value; return value.replace(/'/g, '''); }; ``` ### Pattern 5: Replace eval() with JSON.parse() When data is parsed from JSON responses, replace `eval()` with safe `JSON.parse()`: ```js // AEMCS (with toggle — falls back to eval when toggle is off) if (toggleEnabled) { try { rootData = (window.JSON && JSON.parse) ? JSON.parse(json) : CQ.Ext.decode(json); } catch (e) { throw {message: "Invalid JSON response", cause: e}; } } else { rootData = eval("(" + json + ")"); } // 6.5 / 6.5 LTS (no toggle — always use safe parsing) try { rootData = (window.JSON && JSON.parse) ? JSON.parse(json) : CQ.Ext.decode(json); } catch (e) { throw {message: "Invalid JSON response", cause: e}; } ``` ### Pattern 6: Use pre-sanitized field names from server When the server provides both raw and XSS-safe versions of a field: ```js "displayField": "label_xss" // instead of "label" ```"}}

Never use literal `\n` escape sequences — they render as visible `\n` on GitHub instead of line breaks. ``` BAD — "## Summary\n\nFixed the bug.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [x] tested" GOOD — "## Summary Fixed the bug. ## Test plan - [x] tested" ``` This applies to all GitHub tools: `create_pull_request`, `create_issue`, `add_issue_comment`, `update_issue`, etc. --- ## Rule: scope-discovery.mdc # Scope discovery — GitHub and Git.corp fallback **If the affected JCR path or file cannot be found locally**, do NOT ask the user. Search both sources, verify the candidate is active (not archived), and report the active repo URL. ## Search procedure (single source of truth) 1. **Discover MCP servers** from the project's `mcps/` folder. Identify: - **GitHub (GHEC):** server identifier containing `github` or `gh` (e.g. `user-github.com`) - **Git.corp (Adobe internal):** server identifier containing `git.corp` or `corp` (e.g. `user-git.corp.adobe.com`) 2. **Call `search_code`** on **both** sources (not fallback — parallel). Do not stop at the first hit: - **GitHub:** query `path:{PATH_SEGMENT} org:adobe` (e.g. `path:libs/mcm org:adobe`) - **Git.corp:** query `path:{PATH_SEGMENT}` (e.g. `path:libs/mcm`). AEM/Granite/MCM code was historically on Git.corp and is actively being migrated to GHEC. 3. **Verify archive status for each candidate** before selecting one. For every matching repo, call the source's repo-info tool (`search_repositories` with `org:X name:Y`, or equivalent) and read the `archived` field. - **Active repo found:** prefer it. Report `found on {source}: {URL}`. - **All candidates archived** on one source but the other source has an active match → select the active one. Report `found on {source}: {URL} (upstream on {other source} is archived)`. - **Only archived candidates exist anywhere:** do NOT silently accept them. An archived repo with a recent fix history almost always has a migrated successor. Search the **other** source for the successor using: - the same path segment, plus - the last-known org/repo name as a keyword (e.g. `ui-wcm-admin`), plus - recent PRs on the archived repo that reference the migrated location (check the README, pinned issues, or the last few commit messages for "moved to" / "migrated to" notes). 4. **If no active repo can be found anywhere**, report the archived URL **along with** `archived: true` and stop — do NOT fabricate a migrated URL and do NOT treat the archived repo as the fix target. The caller is responsible for surfacing this as a human-resolution item (see `skills/commit-and-pr/SKILL.md` — the archived-upstream guard). --- ## Rule: toolkit-metadata.mdc # Toolkit metadata When executing a skill, agent, rule, or command that has a `metadata.toolkit` field in its YAML frontmatter, follow these rules. ## 1. Agent window display At the start of each artifact activation, display to the user: ``` v — []() ``` Example: `commit-and-pr v2.1.0 — [cursor-toolkit](https://github.com/Adobe-AEM-Sites/aem-cursor-toolkit)` When a skill delegates to another skill, display the delegation: ``` -> v — []() ``` Example: `-> commit-and-pr v2.1.0 — [cursor-toolkit](https://github.com/Adobe-AEM-Sites/aem-cursor-toolkit)` ## 2. Commit trailers When creating git commits, append a trailer with the primary skill that performed the work: ``` Skill-Version: / v ``` Example: `Skill-Version: cursor-toolkit/fix-accessibility v1.0.0` ## 3. PR footers When creating pull request descriptions, append a footer listing all artifacts that were activated during the workflow, grouped by toolkit: **Single toolkit:** ``` Generated by [](): v, v ``` Example: `Generated by [cursor-toolkit](https://github.com/Adobe-AEM-Sites/aem-cursor-toolkit): fix-accessibility v1.0.0, commit-and-pr v2.1.0` **Multiple toolkits:** ``` Generated by [](): | [](): ``` ## Reading metadata Read all values from the active artifact's frontmatter: - `metadata.toolkit` — the toolkit name (namespace) - `metadata.repository` — the repository URL - `metadata.version` — the artifact's own version Use them as-is — do not hardcode or guess any values. Each artifact has its own independent version. If the active artifact does **not** have `metadata.toolkit`, do nothing — this rule only applies to artifacts that declare toolkit metadata. --- ## Rule: validation-after-fixes.mdc # Validation after fixes When you have applied code fixes (e.g. from a11y, security fixes, or similar): 1. **Run the repo's local build/lint** (e.g. `npm run prepack`, `npm run build`, `npm run lint`). Fix any errors before proceeding. 2. **Then** run any domain-specific validation (e.g. a11y checks, tests). Do not open a PR until both pass. If the repo uses a single script that runs build and lint (e.g. `prepack` in commons-style packages), run that and fix all failures. --- ## Rule: worktree-isolation.mdc # Worktree isolation A plain `git checkout ` mutates the **shared** working tree. If the user (or another agent) is working in the same repo in parallel, that checkout — and any later commit — collides with their state: branch switches yank files out from under each other, uncommitted edits get stashed or lost, and `git commit` fails with `cannot lock ref 'HEAD'`. When a workflow needs a branch other than the one currently checked out, **work in a dedicated worktree**, not the main checkout. ## When this applies - Skills that check out an existing branch to apply changes (e.g. `implement-review-findings` Step 4 checking out a PR's head branch). - Orchestrators and finalizers that create or switch branches (`commit-and-pr`, `resolve-jira-ticket`, `xss-fix-orchestrator`). - Any task the user is likely to run **in parallel** with other work in the same repo (multiple PR reviews, batch fixes). ## How to isolate Prefer the harness primitive when spawning a subagent: - `Agent` tool with `isolation: "worktree"` — the agent gets its own worktree, auto-cleaned if unchanged. Otherwise create one explicitly and operate inside it: ```bash wt="$(mktemp -d)/wt" git worktree add "$wt" # existing branch # or: git worktree add -b "$wt" # ... edit, stage, commit, push from inside "$wt" ... git worktree remove "$wt" ``` Stashes are repo-global, so a `git stash pop` works the same inside a worktree — useful when restoring parked edits. ## Do not - Do **not** `git checkout` a different branch in the main working tree when the user may be mid-task there. Detect the situation (`git branch --show-current` differs from the target, working tree dirty) and use a worktree instead. - Do **not** assume a failed commit (`cannot lock ref 'HEAD'`) means lost work — check `git stash list` and `git reflog` first; the edits are almost always recoverable. --- ## Rule: xss-prevention.mdc # XSS Prevention in AEM ## Critical rules 1. **AEMCS fixes (main/master) MUST be behind a Granite feature toggle** (`CT_SITES-XXXXX`). On-prem 6.5 / 6.5 LTS backports (release/650, release/660) do NOT use toggles — apply the encoding unconditionally. 2. **Fix in the specific affected file**, NOT in shared utilities 3. **Encode right before DOM insertion** — at the output point (constructor, onRender, initComponent, template) 4. **Fall back to the original value** if the toggle is disabled or the XSS API is unavailable (AEMCS only) ## Server-side (JSP) Always include `/libs/foundation/global.jsp` to get the `xssAPI` object. | Output context | Method | Example | |---------------|--------|---------| | HTML text content | `xssAPI.encodeForHTML(val)` | `<%= xssAPI.encodeForHTML(title) %>` | | HTML attribute | `xssAPI.encodeForHTMLAttr(val)` | `title="<%= xssAPI.encodeForHTMLAttr(title) %>"` | | URL / href | `xssAPI.getValidHref(url)` | `href="<%= xssAPI.getValidHref(link) %>"` | | JavaScript string | `xssAPI.encodeForJSString(val)` | `var x = '<%= xssAPI.encodeForJSString(val) %>';` | Never output raw `properties.get(...)` or request parameters without encoding. ```jsp <%@include file="/libs/foundation/global.jsp" %>

<%= xssAPI.encodeForHTML(title) %>

<%= xssAPI.encodeForHTML(title) %> ``` ## Client-side (JavaScript) **`Granite.Sling.XSS` is NOT available in Classic UI.** Classic UI uses `cq.widgets` clientlibs, not Granite clientlibs. In Touch UI / Granite UI pages, `Granite.Sling.XSS` IS available. ### Encoding APIs available in AEM | Context | API | Available in | |---------|-----|-------------| | String config → DOM (name, id, label) | `CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(val)` | Classic UI widget pages | | HTML content (rich markup) | `Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS.sanitizeHtml(val)` | Classic UI and Granite UI pages | | URL / iframe src | `sanitizeURL()` helper using `new URL(path, document.baseURI)` | Any JS context | | Single-quoted HTML attribute | `value.replace(/'/g, ''')` | Any JS context | | JSON response parsing (replace eval) | `JSON.parse(json)` or `CQ.Ext.decode(json)` | Any JS context | ### Feature toggle and availability guard boilerplate (AEMCS only) Feature toggles are required **only for AEMCS** (main/master branch). For 6.5 / 6.5 LTS backports, skip the toggle and apply the encoding unconditionally. ```js // AEMCS — wrap in toggle guard if (Granite && Granite.Toggles && Granite.Toggles.isEnabled("CT_SITES-XXXXX")) { // XSS fix here — replace XXXXX with the actual Jira ticket key } // 6.5 / 6.5 LTS — no toggle, apply directly cfg.name = CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(cfg.name); ``` Standard availability checks (AEMCS): ```js var toggleEnabled = Granite && Granite.Toggles && Granite.Toggles.isEnabled("CT_SITES-XXXXX"); var xssAvailable = CQ && CQ.shared && CQ.shared.XSS; var sanitizeHtmlAvailable = Granite && Granite.UI && Granite.UI.Foundation && Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS; if (toggleEnabled && xssAvailable) { cfg.name = CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(cfg.name); } ``` ## Fix patterns (from commit history) The patterns below show the AEMCS form (with toggle guard). For 6.5 / 6.5 LTS backports, remove the toggle check and apply the encoding unconditionally (keep the API availability check). ### Pattern 1: Sanitize config properties with CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue() For string config properties that get inserted into DOM (name, id, inputType, hrefTarget, prefix/suffix labels): ```js // AEMCS (with toggle) if (toggleEnabled && xssAvailable) { cfg.name = CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(cfg.name); } // 6.5 / 6.5 LTS (no toggle) if (xssAvailable) { cfg.name = CQ.shared.XSS.getXSSValue(cfg.name); } ``` ### Pattern 2: Sanitize HTML content with Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS.sanitizeHtml() For properties that contain HTML markup (e.g. `this.html`, rich text fields): ```js // AEMCS (with toggle) if (toggleEnabled && sanitizeHtmlAvailable) { this.html = Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS.sanitizeHtml(this.html); } // 6.5 / 6.5 LTS (no toggle) if (sanitizeHtmlAvailable) { this.html = Granite.UI.Foundation.XSS.sanitizeHtml(this.html); } ``` ### Pattern 3: Sanitize URLs with a sanitizeURL helper For URLs loaded into iframes or used as src/href: ```js sanitizeURL: function(path) { try { var url = new URL(path, document.baseURI); if (url.origin !== "null") { return url.pathname + url.search; } else { return ""; } } catch(error) { return ""; } } ``` ### Pattern 4: Encode single quotes for HTML attribute context When values are interpolated into single-quoted HTML attributes (e.g. `src='...'`): ```js var encodeForHTMLAttr = function(value) { if (typeof value !== 'string') return value; return value.replace(/'/g, '''); }; ``` ### Pattern 5: Replace eval() with JSON.parse() When data is parsed from JSON responses, replace `eval()` with safe `JSON.parse()`: ```js // AEMCS (with toggle — falls back to eval when toggle is off) if (toggleEnabled) { try { rootData = (window.JSON && JSON.parse) ? JSON.parse(json) : CQ.Ext.decode(json); } catch (e) { throw {message: "Invalid JSON response", cause: e}; } } else { rootData = eval("(" + json + ")"); } // 6.5 / 6.5 LTS (no toggle — always use safe parsing) try { rootData = (window.JSON && JSON.parse) ? JSON.parse(json) : CQ.Ext.decode(json); } catch (e) { throw {message: "Invalid JSON response", cause: e}; } ``` ### Pattern 6: Use pre-sanitized field names from server When the server provides both raw and XSS-safe versions of a field: ```js "displayField": "label_xss" // instead of "label" ```"}}
